sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: b) For a finite group$X$, let$_1(X)$denote the totality of the degrees of all irre\-du\-cible complex characters of$X$with allowance for their multiplicities. Suppose that$_1(G)= _1(H)$for groups$G$and$H$. Clearly, then$|G|=|H|$. Is it true that$H$is soluble if$G$is soluble? It is known that$H$is a Frobenius group if$G$is a Frobenius group.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
